skill.name: pinia
skill.description: Pinia guidance for building type-safe Vue stores, including state, getters, actions, plugins, testing, SSR, Nuxt, and HMR.
skill.summary: Build and maintain type-safe Pinia stores for Vue applications.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, network, filesystem, credential, and heuristic combination findings, but review found markdown documentation examples rather than executable skill logic. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration intent, or runtime automation was found; the residual risk is low because examples mention browser storage, environment checks, install commands, and source URLs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by fenced and inline markdown code examples. The reviewed lines document Pinia, Nuxt, HMR, and testing usage and do not define executable hooks, scripts, or command runners in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown metadata or documentation snippets. No executable wrapper, shell API, or user-controlled command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Browser Storage Examples Are Educational
low_findings.description: Several sensitive-data findings come from examples that use localStorage or VueUse composables to show Pinia persistence and hydration patterns. These examples are not skill behavior and do not transmit stored values to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The localStorage usage is visible in ordinary Pinia documentation examples. It could affect an application if copied with sensitive data, but the skill itself performs no browser storage access.

low_findings.title: Environment and Path Indicators Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The environment variable and path traversal detections are false positives from normal frontend examples, including a NODE_ENV development check and relative import paths. No .env file reading, credential loading, or filesystem traversal behavior is implemented by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are TypeScript snippets in markdown. They use conventional frontend imports and a development-mode condition, not filesystem access logic.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Source References
low_findings.description: Network findings are documentation links to Pinia, GitHub, and related resources. They are not fetch calls, webhook destinations, telemetry endpoints, or instructions to send user data over the network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear as source attribution or recommended documentation links. No code path performs network requests from the skill.

content.user_title: Build Pinia Stores for Vue Apps
content.value_statement: Vue teams need consistent state patterns across components, tests, SSR, and Nuxt apps. This skill provides focused Pinia guidance with examples for stores, plugins, hydration, and testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains Option Stores and Setup Stores with state, getters, and actions.
Guides reactivity-safe destructuring with storeToRefs and direct action use.
Documents Pinia plugins, custom properties, custom options, and TypeScript augmentation.
Covers testing with fresh Pinia instances, createTestingPinia, stubs, spies, and mocked stores.
Provides SSR, Nuxt, hydration, outside-component usage, and HMR guidance.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not an executable Pinia generator.
It is based on Pinia v3.0.4 content generated on 2026-01-28.
It does not inspect a project unless the user provides files or context.
It cannot verify runtime behavior without running the application tests.

use_cases.title: Create a Store Pattern
use_cases.description: Design a Pinia store using Setup Store or Option Store syntax with typed state, getters, and actions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Store Tests
use_cases.description: Set up isolated Pinia tests with createPinia, createTestingPinia, action stubs, and typed mocked stores.

use_cases.title: Fix SSR and Nuxt Usage
use_cases.description: Apply Pinia guidance for auto imports, route middleware, hydration, and avoiding cross-request state pollution.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Basic Store
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Pinia store for this Vue feature. Include state, getters, actions, and explain whether Setup Store or Option Store fits best.

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Reactivity
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component and store usage. Show where to use storeToRefs, where actions can be destructured, and what could break reactivity.

prompt_templates.title: Test Store Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Design tests for this Pinia store and its consuming component. Include initial state, action stubbing choices, and typed mock guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Nuxt SSR Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Nuxt Pinia setup for SSR safety. Check middleware usage, hydration order, plugin registration, and cross-request state risks.

output_examples.input: A cart feature needs shared state, derived totals, and a checkout action.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a Setup Store, separates cart items from computed totals, keeps checkout as an action, and notes where other stores may be called safely.

output_examples.input: A component test needs a user store with a known logged-in state.
output_examples.output: The response describes using createTestingPinia with initial state, choosing whether actions are stubbed, and asserting component behavior through the rendered UI.

output_examples.input: A Nuxt middleware reads an auth store before redirecting users.
output_examples.output: The response explains when to pass the Pinia instance, how Nuxt auto-imports affect usage, and how to avoid SSR context mistakes.

best_practices: Prefer Setup Stores for complex logic, composables, watchers, and reusable state behavior.
Use storeToRefs when destructuring state or getters, and destructure actions directly.
Create fresh Pinia instances for tests and SSR requests to avoid shared state leaks.
anti_patterns: Do not call stores at module scope before Pinia is installed.
Do not read circular store state during setup in two stores.
Do not return non-serializable DOM refs from Setup Stores.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Vue applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides Pinia-specific guidance and examples for stores, tests, plugins, SSR, Nuxt, and HMR.

faq.question: Does it support both Pinia store styles?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Option Stores and Setup Stores, including when Setup Stores are preferred.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains inferred state types, explicit state interfaces, store typing, and plugin augmentation patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with Nuxt?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Nuxt module setup, auto imports, middleware usage, plugins, and SSR-aware data fetching.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers createPinia, createTestingPinia, initial state, action stubs, spies, plugins, and typed mocked stores.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access my files by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is markdown documentation and does not include executable scripts or automation code.
